topic: Fire Warlock vs Fire Immunes
nugget: Fire Warlock dealing with fire immunes in Hell: equip an Obedience runeword (Hel + Ko + Thul + Eth + Fal in any 5-socket polearm) on the merc. Obedience grants -25% enemy fire resistance, which combined with -fire-res charms can break most fire immunes. For the worst offenders (Act 4 demons, Hephasto, Mephisto), bind a Demon Consume buff that adds Magic damage and run a couple of Magic-tree skills (Echoing Strike, Abyss) as a backup so you are never hard-stuck.

topic: Warlock Hephasto Conviction Merc
nugget: When farming Hell as a Warlock, reroll Act 4 Hephasto until he spawns with Conviction aura, then hire him as your mercenary. Conviction lowers enemy fire/cold/lightning resistance by up to 125%, which breaks the vast majority of fire immunes for a Fire Warlock. This is the cheapest Hell-viable answer to fire immunity for any elemental Warlock build and is also why magic-build Warlocks tolerate fire immunes more easily.

topic: Warlock Magic Build Pivot for Fire Immunes
nugget: If a Fire Warlock keeps getting walled by fire immunes in Hell, the most reliable pivot is the Magic skill tree (Abyss / Echoing Strike). Magic damage in D2R has no monster immunity, so a Magic Warlock plows through fire immunes (and cold/lightning immunes) with no extra setup. The Abyss runeword in a 4-socket weapon is the standard early Hell-farming choice and pairs well with a Spirit shield for FCR. Demon-tree summons (bound Hephasto, bound Defiler) also still carry on a Magic Warlock.

topic: Warlock Echoing Strike vs Fire Immunes
nugget: Echoing Strike is a Magic-damage Warlock skill that ignores fire immunity entirely. It scales hard once you have +skills and FCR, and is one of the strongest single-target answers to fire-immune bosses (Hephasto, Mephisto, Diablo) for any Warlock that has trouble with fire immunes in Hell. Many Hell-farming Warlocks keep ES on a swap weapon even on a primarily Fire build for exactly this reason.

topic: Early Hell Warlock: ES vs Magic vs Demon comparison
nugget: For early Hell farming as a Warlock, the three main paths are: (1) Echoing Strike (ES) with a cursed bound Hephasto demon — this is widely considered the strongest; run Insight polearm on merc, dual Spirit (weapon+shield), aim for 75%% FCR and dual leech rings. Heph with Conviction aura 2-shots most packs on /players3. (2) Magic/Abyss — very comfy and safe, Abyss deals AoE magic damage that has no monster immunity in D2R, so you clear everything including fire/cold/lightning immunes. Spirit + Spirit and basic FCR gear is enough to solo farm Andy or CS. (3) Pure Demon/Summoner — Heph Defiler walking sim: stack ptopaz in 4os chest + 3os helm for MF, use dual Spirit, and let your bound demons do all the killing. ES has the highest ceiling but Abyss is the smoothest start with budget gear.

topic: Warlock ES + Bound Hephasto Early Hell Farming
nugget: Echoing Strike Warlock with a bound Hephasto demon is easy mode for early Hell. Bind a Hephasto from Act 4 with a cursed aura (reroll 10-15 times if needed for Conviction). Gear: Insight polearm on Act 2 merc for mana, dual Spirit (sword+shield) for FCR and +skills, aim for 75%% FCR breakpoint. Dual leech rings help sustain. Heph tanks everything via Blood Oath and 2-shots packs on /players3. For bosses, swap Defiler for Goatmen for Crushing Blow. Stack ptopaz in chest/helm for MF once stable. CS and Andy are the best early farming spots.

topic: Warlock 1-Point Bind Demon Investment
nugget: Every warlock build should invest at least 1 point in Bind Demon at level 30 — the community consensus is overwhelming. Even with just 1 point in Bind Demon, 1 in Demon Mastery, and 1 in Blood Oath (3 points total), the impact is enormous. Find a demon with a cursed modifier and a useful aura (Act 4 Hephasto with Conviction is ideal, reroll if needed). The bound demon tanks everything via Blood Oath, can replace your merc entirely, and with 1 point in Consume you get a damage/HP/faster-run-walk buff. For a Magic Warlock with leftover points after maxing Miasma tree, this is the highest-value investment possible. Even on SSF with limited +skills (~+5), bound Hephasto carries through Baal wave 2 magic immunes because the demon deals physical damage.
